title: ಸಿಂಹ ಮತ್ತು ಇಲಿ
summary: A short story built around kindness, apology, and unexpected help, with simple verbs and concrete imagery.

segments.english: A big lion was sleeping in the forest.
A small mouse ran across it.
The lion woke up and caught the mouse.

segments.kannada: ದಯವಿಟ್ಟು ನನ್ನನ್ನು ಬಿಡಿ ಎಂದು ಇಲಿ ಬೇಡಿಕೊಂಡಿತು.
segments.english: The mouse begged, “Please let me go.”

segments.kannada: ಒಂದು ದಿನ ನಾನು ನಿಮಗೆ ಸಹಾಯ ಮಾಡಬಹುದು ಎಂದು ಅದು ಹೇಳಿತು.
segments.english: It said that one day it might help the lion.

segments.kannada: ನಂತರ ಸಿಂಹ ಬಲೆಗೆ ಸಿಕ್ಕಿತು ಮತ್ತು ಗರ್ಜಿಸಿತು.
segments.english: Later the lion was trapped in a net and roared.

segments.kannada: ಇಲಿ ಬಂದು ಕಗ್ಗಂಟನ್ನು ಕಚ್ಚಿ ಸಿಂಹವನ್ನು ಬಿಡಿಸಿತು.
segments.english: The mouse came, gnawed the ropes, and freed the lion.
Even someone small can be a good friend and help.

inlineGlossary.note: Useful for moving from sleep to attention.
Common in polite or desperate requests.
A concrete noun that appears in many folk stories.
A high-value verb for rescue situations.

vocabulary.note: Useful for transition from sleep to attention.
Common in polite or desperate requests.
A concrete noun that appears in many folk stories.
Helpful for stories involving tying or trapping.
A high-value verb for rescue situations.

comprehensionQuestions.prompt: Why did the mouse ask the lion to let it go?

options.text: It wanted to race the lion.
It hoped it could help the lion one day.
It wanted the lion to follow it into the forest.

comprehensionQuestions.explanation: The mouse promises future help when it begs for mercy.

comprehensionQuestions.prompt: What problem did the lion face later in the story?

options.text: It got trapped in a net.
It got lost during a race.
It could not find the mouse.

comprehensionQuestions.explanation: The turning point is the lion getting stuck in a trap.

comprehensionQuestions.prompt: What lesson does the story emphasize?

options.text: Only strong animals can help others.
Small friends can still offer meaningful help.
Asking for help is always a weakness.

comprehensionQuestions.explanation: The mouse proves that size does not decide usefulness or kindness.
